title: Overcome Objections — The Boss Battles
intro:
STEP 7 OF 8 · MODULE 7

Overcome Objections — The Boss Battles

Mission: Convert hesitation into clarity. Objections are not rejection — they are uncertainty wearing a costume.

OBJECTIVE
RESOLVE HESITATION
FRAMEWORK
8 BOSS BATTLES
PRIMARY KPI
1ST-OBJ CLOSE %
FAILURE MODE
ARGUING

sections.title: The Objection Translation Matrix
sections.html:
The Objection Translation Matrix

Objections are never literal. They are always a stand-in for a deeper uncertainty the customer either can’t name or doesn’t want to admit. The advisor’s job is to translate the surface objection into the real one — then respond to that.

What Customer SaysWhat They Actually MeanAdvisor ObjectiveMove
“I’m going to sell the car”“This feels like throwing money at a sinking ship”Reframe the alternativeBoss 1 — cost-of-replacement math
“Price is too high”“I’m not sure this is real value”Anchor to what they’re actually buyingBoss 2 — anti-lowest-price brag
“Don’t have the money”“Not today — but I might if there’s a way”Reframe from cost to timingBoss 3 — financing pivot
“Talk to my spouse”“I need backup / a delay”Get the spouse on the same callBoss 4 — three-way bypass
“I’ll wait”“I’m unsure of urgency”Clarify safety vs. maintenanceBoss 5 — risk reframe
“You’re always selling”“I don’t fully trust you”Shift from salesperson to fiduciaryBoss 6 — Pro Obligation frame
“I need my car today”“Logistics fear”Solve logistics first, then return to repairBoss 7 — transport offer
“Parts and labor breakdown?”“I want to comparison-shop”Refuse the apples-to-apples frameBoss 8 — protection-program reframe
BOSS BATTLES · ALL EIGHT EXIST IN THE ADVISOR TRAINING SYSTEM PLAYBOOK This module is the strategic layer above the scripts — the WHY and the COACH. Reference the playbook for the exact word-for-word counters.

sections.title: The Five Universal Rules of Objection Handling
sections.html:
The Five Universal Rules of Objection Handling

  1. Never argue. Arguing tells the customer they were wrong to feel the way they feel. Even if you win the argument, you’ve lost the relationship.
  2. Acknowledge first, always. “I completely understand” / “I hear you” / “Fair enough.” This is not concession — it’s permission to be heard.
  3. Find the real concern. Use clarifying questions: “Is it more than you expected, or are you comparing it to another estimate?” Information beats persuasion.
  4. Pivot to value or risk, never to discount. The instant you discount in response to an objection, you have trained the customer to objection-shop you forever.
  5. Re-ask for the close. Every objection-handled exchange must end with another move toward authorization. Otherwise you’ve just had a conversation.

sections.title: Coach’s Corner — The Boss Battle Drill
sections.html:
Coach’s Corner — The Boss Battle Drill

COACH’S CORNERRun a weekly Boss Rush.

15-minute team standup. Coach throws random Boss lines. Advisors counter in turn, no notes. Score 1–3 per counter. Track who has cleared all eight in live calls within 30 days. Post the leaderboard. This is the fastest path to Level 4 (PRO).

1st-Objection Close %
≥ 60%
% of presentations where authorization happens after the first objection counter.
Missed-Sale Recovered %
≥ 25%
% of declined safety items that get reopened and authorized within 7 days.
Boss Coverage
8/8
Each advisor has defeated all 8 bosses in live calls.
